name: # CMU dict corrections — these entries override the CMU Pronouncing Dictionary
# Each name's CMU pronunciations are fully replaced by the override(s).
# Use multiple rows for the same name to provide multiple pronunciations.
# CMU says JH EH1 R IY0 (same as Jerry) — actually pronounced "JEER"
# Hugh: CMU lists Y UW1 as alt pronunciation
# Hugh always has the initial H sound
# CMU lists K EH1 R AH0 N as alt — should only be SH sound
# CMU lists S AY1 as alt — should keep the TS onset
# --- Bad CMU dict entries (wrong alt pronunciations) ---
# Cian: Irish name pronounced "KEE-an" (same as Kian)
# CMU says SH IY1 N — which pulls Shean/Sheen into Kian's group
# Hugo: CMU lists Y UW1 G OW0 as alt (no H) — causes merge with Yugo
# Tao: Chinese name with T onset — CMU lists D AW1 as alt
# Duell: surname/given name — CMU lists JH UW1 L as alt
# Subword produces Y AA1 S EH1 N which matches Jasen
# Subword produces JH EY1 D AH0 N
Tjaden
pronunciation: T Y AH1 D AH0 N

name: # Ngai: Cantonese name with nasal onset
# Subword produces SH IY1 L AH0
# Subword produces W AH1 N / W AA1 N which matches Juan
# Subword produces Y AA1 M IY1 which matches bad subword results for Jamee/Jame
# --- Genuine sound-alikes that should not merge (different origins) ---
# Both produce HH AY1 M IY0 — override to distinguish
# CMU says K AY1 — wrong; should have CH onset
# CMU gives D IH1 L AH0 N (same as Dylan) — Punjabi stress differs
# CMU gives K AO1 R IY0 (2 syllables
# Subword produces OW1 AH0 N matching Owen
# CMU gives F EY1 (same as Faye)
# CMU gives K UW1 P ER0 (same as Cooper)
# These names are correctly force-merged to their target
# CMU/subword pronunciations act as "magnets" pulling in unrelated names.
# Override to match the target's pronunciation to stop false attraction.
# Keven: variant of Kevin — CMU gives K IY1 V AH0 N which pulls in Kievan
# Override to match Kevin's K EH1 V IH0 N
# Ginna: variant of Gina — CMU gives JH IH1 N N AA1 which pulls in Jinnah
# Override to match Gina's JH IY1 N AH0
